meta.title: Post — Inkwell Journal essay detail
meta.description: A single essay from the Inkwell Journal posts collection, with its related topics resolved live from the posts-to-tags relation.

item.title: The second draft is the real one
item.excerpt: Everything on this journal is published twice: once to find out what we think, once to say it properly.
item.body:
First drafts are for discovering the argument. They meander, they hedge, they bury the point in paragraph four. That is fine — their job is excavation, not presentation.

The second draft starts from one question: what is the single sentence this piece exists to deliver? Everything that does not serve that sentence gets cut, no matter how much we liked writing it.

Our average post loses forty percent of its words between drafts. Readers never miss them.

image.alt: Handwritten manuscript pages with edits and crossed-out lines

item.author: June Park
